event: vaginal bleeding soaked 1 sanitary pad daily
two previous antenatal visits with midwife
referred to obstetrician for first ultrasound
ultrasound showed enlarged uterus with snowstorm appearance
mass on left labia minora (3x3x2 cm)
vacuum curettage performed with 3000 cc blood loss
fragile tissue at base of hematoma
histopathological examination of labial mass and curettage
cytotrophoblasts and syncytiotrophoblasts with excessive proliferation
histopathologic diagnosis: complete mole with excessive trophoblastic proliferation
partial hydatidiform mole in vagina due to suspected embolism
uterine curettage specimen: partial hydatidiform mole
hypoxia due to massive blood loss
hemoglobin increased to 9 g/dL after transfusion
